response: The internet traces its roots to U.S. Department of Defense networking research, with ARPANET’s first message sent in 1969; the shift to TCP/IP in 1983 laid the technical foundation for today’s global network. Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 creation of the World Wide Web—introducing HTML, URLs, and HTTP—and the 1993 release of the Mosaic browser opened the system to the public. After the late-1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash, survivors such as Amazon and Google flourished, while the mid-2000s rise of social media (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube), smartphones (iPhone, 2007), and cloud services (AWS, 2006) made the web pervasive. Today the internet links more than 5 billion people and underpins commerce, communication, and entertainment worldwide.
